step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the selection of option C. Brachytherapy does involve the direct insertion of radioactive substances into or very close to the affected tissue, providing highly accurate and effective local radiation treatment. Additionally, this approach reduces unnecessary exposure to surrounding healthy areas, making it distinctively different from external radiation methods where beams pass through the entire length of the patient. Therefore, option C succinctly captures the key feature of brachytherapy.
